items.text: The win-probability bar on every pick now shows a hover/tap legend that labels each marker: the white line is the model's point estimate, the orange marker is Bovada's implied probability, the green band is the 80% credible range, and the gray center line is even odds (50% coin-flip). The tooltip also surfaces concrete numbers attributed to the favored team — e.g. '54% (51–57%) — STL · slight lean' — using the asymmetric CI bounds rather than a symmetric ±. On the game detail page, the CI header now reads 'Win-prob lean' and shows the full attributed range inline; the bar itself is tappable/keyboard-focusable. Accessibility: the bar has a tab stop and aria-label. Single MLB games are high-variance; the calibrated model output spans roughly 43–57% — treat every pick as a lean, not a lock.

Fanatics is now included in the Book Comparison on each pick's detail page. Where Fanatics offers a line, you'll see its moneyline and total prices alongside its de-vigged market probability, model EV, edge, and breakeven price — the same full treatment every other US book receives. Fanatics is also counted in the cross-book '+EV' pill ('N of M books +EV').

Caesars was not appearing in the Book Comparison despite having live lines. The data pipeline was ingesting Caesars under a legacy key ('williamhill_us') that the serving store's book filter didn't recognize. The filter has been updated and Caesars will show correctly after the next odds refresh.

Book Comparison now shows a model breakeven price for each pick — the price at which the model's edge reaches exactly zero. A green chip means the current book price is still better than breakeven; red means the line has moved past the point where the model rates it +EV. Pinnacle's sharp no-vig fair value is always shown alongside for context. This is a transparency tool: our models have no demonstrated market edge, so 'breakeven' is model-relative, not a guarantee.

The Performance page's Win % and CLV stats now measure the side the model actually favored. Previously they reported the raw home-team win rate (Moneyline) and Over rate (Totals) regardless of which side the model picked, so 'Win %' was a base rate rather than the model's accuracy. Win % is now the share of the model's directional picks that won, and Closing Line Value is measured in the pick's direction. Tooltips were rewritten to match. Brier Score (calibration) is unchanged.

The 'Yesterday' badge on the front page now shows the real result of the featured pick. Previously, the badge always appeared green even after a loss — the system was surfacing whichever of yesterday's picks happened to win, not the actual pick that was shown. The pick selection now uses the same logic as today's featured pick, and the badge correctly shows green for a win, red for a loss, and gray for a game that hasn't been settled yet.

The models behind the picks have been upgraded to leak-free versions, and the 'why this pick' explanations now reflect them. The previous models' driver explanations were dominated by a bullpen metric that was partly computed from the game being predicted — an internal data artifact, not a real signal — so nearly every game over-credited the bullpen. The new models remove that leak and are far leaner; the displayed drivers (team strength/ELO, run-based win expectation, and pitching/offense form) now show what the model actually weighs. Picks and probabilities may shift as a result. As always, treat each pick as a lean, not a lock.

The Book Comparison on each pick's detail page was showing odds captured after the game started, which can't be bet. Lines now freeze at the last pre-game snapshot, so every price displayed is one that was actually available before first pitch.

Win probabilities are now calibrated to be honest about how close most MLB games are. We checked every model win-prob against what actually happened this season and found the numbers were overconfident — picks shown near 75–85% were really landing closer to a coin flip. We've corrected the calibration so the displayed probability now reflects the true rate (when we say 55%, the home team wins about 55%). Expect tighter, more honest numbers: single baseball games are high-variance, so treat each pick as a lean, not a lock.

Settings page now lets you set an initial deposit amount — the starting bankroll you track growth against. Once set, the EV Tracker can show bankroll growth % alongside net P&L. The field saves directly to your account, not just this device.

Model reasoning now covers both H2H and Totals signals in one view. A toggle on the featured pick card and on game detail pages lets you switch between win-probability drivers and run-total drivers — so you can see exactly what's pushing each market, not just the headline pick.

The win probability shown in the model write-up now matches the number displayed in the probability bar. Previously the narrative was pulling from a single sub-model output (68%) rather than the full ensemble (74%); both figures now agree.

Pick write-ups now stay in sync with the served pick. The plain-English explanation and feature drivers are regenerated alongside each day's predictions, so the reasoning you read always matches the pick currently shown — no more stale morning write-up lingering after the pick updates post-lineup.
Teams directory launched at /teams — all 30 MLB teams organized by division with current win-loss record, win percentage, and win/loss streak. Click any team to open the detail page.

Team detail pages now live at /team/[team] — each page shows the team's ELO trend over time, platoon splits (xwOBA/wOBA vs LHP and RHP), recent form (last 10 games), upcoming schedule with probable starters, and a key-stats grid covering run differential, Pythagorean win expectation, offensive and pitching xwOBA, and bullpen quality.

Players directory launched at /players — searchable and filterable lists of all active batters and pitchers, with tabs to switch between the two. Batter columns include AVG, HR, wOBA, and xwOBA; pitcher columns include ERA, IP, K, and xwOBA-against. Click any row to open the player profile.
Player profile pages now display age, height, and weight in the profile header alongside position and team.
Players currently on the injured list show a red IL badge with the date their stint began.

Pick details now include a posterior confidence tier (low / medium / high) derived from the model's calibrated uncertainty interval relative to the edge — high-confidence picks have a tight posterior relative to their edge size.

Pick detail pages now show a model conviction score and an 80% win-probability credible interval for h2h picks — a visual bar displaying how wide the model's uncertainty is, with the Bovada implied probability marked for comparison. Conviction and CI are confidence signals; they reflect model certainty, not a recommendation to bet.

The probability bar on the dashboard now shows a hover tooltip with the exact model win%, Bovada win%, and 80% credible interval range for moneyline picks — so the visual is clean at a glance but the numbers are always one hover away.

Book Comparison panel added to every game detail page — select from BetMGM, Caesars, FanDuel, DraftKings, Bovada, or Pinnacle and see that book's moneyline and totals prices alongside the model's probability, the book's de-vigged implied market %, and expected value per $1. Pinnacle is always shown as the sharp low-vig reference anchor. This is a market transparency tool; our models have no demonstrated market edge and all bets are manual.

You can now reset your password from the login page — click "Forgot your password?", enter your email to receive a reset code, then use the code to set a new password. The link appears below the Sign In button.

Fixed a bug where the "Why this pick" write-up could name the wrong team as the model's pick. The narrative now states each team's win probability by name (e.g. SEA: 21%, BAL: 79%) so the model-backed side matches the chip label. A guard now blocks generation if the pick direction is internally inconsistent.

Pick detail pages now show a CLV-confidence bar for both the moneyline and totals markets — a probability bar showing P(closing line moves toward this side) with an 80% credible interval. The totals bar is shown as low-information (the totals CLV model has no demonstrated open-to-close predictive signal at this stage). Both bars are transparency tools framing line-value confidence, not bet recommendations.
Pick detail pages now show model reasoning: a plain-English narrative plus signed feature attribution bars grouped by category (starting pitcher, bullpen, team offense, park & weather).
Featured pick card on the homepage surfaces the model's narrative and top feature drivers; the static probability summary is hidden when a narrative is available.
Blog posts written in the admin editor are now publicly visible at /blog and /blog/[slug]; the homepage surfaces the latest published post as a teaser below the hero.
Dashboard picks now reflect your local date — users in CST, MT, and PT time zones see the correct day's picks after 11 PM without waiting for midnight ET.
Admin pipeline monitoring page now shows real-time Dagster run history and model freshness from production; mock data removed.
Settings page added under the gear icon in the top-right nav — shows verified email, subscription tier, and sign-out controls.
FAQ and Contact pages published — 15 questions across four categories covering model methodology, platform mechanics, and responsible use.
Admin sub-nav link is now shown only to users in the admin Cognito group, replacing the previous hardcoded email list.
Umpire feed repair: home plate umpire zscore null rate dropped from 34.6% to 1.1% after wiring the UmpScorecards JSON loader and moving assignment ingestion to the afternoon lineup path.
Footer now visible on all public pages; previous layout bug caused it to render below the viewport fold on short pages.
Request Access CTA updated to a direct-email link so beta applicants can reach us immediately without a form intermediary.
